dep.name: sendme
dep.purpose: Reference implementation and transport patterns for iroh-based secure data movement.

dep.name: iroh
dep.purpose: Peer transport, NAT traversal, relay fallback, and encrypted connectivity primitives.

dep.name: iroh-blobs
dep.purpose: Reference protocol surface reused from the upstream ecosystem where appropriate.

dep.name: tokio
dep.purpose: Async runtime for sockets, tasks, shutdown, and backpressure handling.

dep.name: tracing
dep.purpose: Structured logs and span-based diagnostic evidence.

dep.name: tracing-subscriber
dep.purpose: Structured log formatting and environment-based filtering.

dep.name: clap
dep.purpose: CLI configuration for local client and remote server modes.

dep.name: anyhow
dep.purpose: Application-level error composition during early implementation.

constraint.reason: Keep parity with the current sendme rust-version requirement.
Anchor the first implementation to the currently published upstream surface.
Async runtime compatibility across websocket and transport modules.

module-level.focus: Fast deterministic unit and contract checks around config, auth, and session logic.

wave-level.focus: Integration checks for WSS handshake, session startup, and proxy request flow.

phase-level.focus: Broader transport, shutdown, and regression confidence.

Testing.mocking-policy: Prefer local fakes, loopback sockets, and deterministic transport doubles over broad mocks.

Observability.redaction: Never log secrets, auth tokens, proxy credentials, or raw proxied payload bytes.

DeliveryShape.shape: Rust binary with at least two modes: local proxy client and remote WSS/iroh relay-capable server.
